1: While the focus of this appendix and much of current LLM interaction is text-based,
2: the field is rapidly moving towards multimodal models that can process and generate
3: information across different modalities (text, images, audio, video, etc.). Multimodal
4: prompting involves using a combination of inputs to guide the model. This refers to
5: using multiple input formats instead of just text.
7: Example: Providing an image of a diagram and asking the model to explain the
8: process shown in the diagram (Image Input + Text Prompt). Or providing an image
9: and asking the model to generate a descriptive caption (Image Input + Text
11: As multimodal capabilities become more sophisticated, prompting techniques will
12: evolve to effectively leverage these combined inputs and outputs.
14: Becoming a skilled prompt engineer is an iterative process that involves continuous
15: learning and experimentation. Several valuable best practices are worth reiterating
18: Provide Examples: Providing one or few-shot examples is one of the most
19: effective ways to guide the model.
21: Design with Simplicity: Keep your prompts concise, clear, and easy to
22: understand. Avoid unnecessary jargon or overly complex phrasing.
24: Be Specific about the Output: Clearly define the desired format, length, style,
25: and content of the model's response.
27: Use Instructions over Constraints: Focus on telling the model what you want it
28: to do rather than what you don't want it to do.
30: Control the Max Token Length: Use model configurations or explicit prompt
31: instructions to manage the length of the generated output.
33: Use Variables in Prompts: For prompts used in applications, use variables to
34: make them dynamic and reusable, avoiding hardcoding specific values.
36: Experiment with Input Formats and Writing Styles: Try different ways of
37: phrasing your prompt (question, statement, instruction) and experiment with
38: different tones or styles to see what yields the best results.
40: For Few-Shot Prompting with Classification Tasks, Mix Up the Classes:
41: Randomize the order of examples from different categories to prevent overfitting.
